data.content: I can see the project — it's an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. The express delivery test is failing (returns `10.00` but expects `15.00`). Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically on every save:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText: The user wants hot reload for their .NET test project. I can use `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change. Let me start that up.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-0013c880420147b68a046a37de2d4f93 && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content: There's a missing `using System;` causing a build error. Let me fix that so the watch session can run:
data.reasoningText:
There's a missing `using System;` directive. The `ArgumentException` type requires it. Let me fix that — but wait, the user said they have failing tests and want hot reload. They didn't ask me to fix the tests. Let me add the missing `using System;` so the project at least compiles, then the watch will be running.

Actually, the user said "I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster." So they want me to set up hot reload so they can iterate. The project doesn't compile right now due to the missing `using System;`. I should fix this compilation error so the watch session works properly, then leave the actual test failure for them to fix with the hot reload.


I'll add the missing directive so the code compiles and the watch can pick up changes as they work through the test failures.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running. It detected the fix and re-ran tests automatically. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expected `15.00`, got `10.00`

The watch is active in shell `watch` — every time you save a file, tests will automatically re-run. The failing test is in `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` line 23 where `"express"` returns `10.00m` instead of `15.00m`.
